questions: when should you file for bankruptcy?
answers: Bankruptcy is a legal process where individuals or businesses that cannot repay their debts seek relief from some or all of their financial obligations through court proceedings.
Bankruptcy holo legal process jeta manush ba company follow kore jokhon tara debt pay korte pare na. Court tader assets ar debts manage kore.
There are different types like Chapter 7 (liquidation), Chapter 11 (reorganization for businesses), and Chapter 13 (repayment plan for individuals) in the US system.
Filing bankruptcy can provide debt relief and a fresh start, but it severely impacts your credit score for 7-10 years and may result in losing some assets.
